skills.name: HTML
skills.description: The foundational markup language used to structure content on the web, defining elements like headings, paragraphs, forms, and media.
skills.notes: HTML provides the skeleton of web pages. Learn semantic tags, accessibility (ARIA), forms, inputs, and proper document structure for SEO and maintainability.

skills.name: CSS
skills.description: Styling language used to design and layout web pages, including colors, spacing, responsiveness, and animations.
skills.notes: Covers Flexbox, Grid, responsive design, media queries, and modern UI techniques. Also includes frameworks like Tailwind CSS for faster styling workflows.

skills.name: JavaScript
skills.description: Core programming language of the web used to add interactivity, handle events, and communicate with servers.
skills.notes: Learn DOM manipulation, asynchronous programming (Promises, async/await), event handling, and working with APIs. JavaScript is essential for both frontend and backend (Node.js).

skills.name: Frontend Frameworks
skills.description: Libraries and frameworks like React that help build dynamic, component-based user interfaces efficiently.
skills.notes: Focus on React fundamentals, state management, hooks, routing, and component architecture. Tailwind CSS can be used alongside for styling modern UI quickly.

skills.name: Version Control & Collaboration
skills.description: Tools and workflows used to manage code changes, collaborate with teams, and maintain project history.
skills.notes: Learn Git fundamentals (commit, branch, merge), and platforms like GitHub. Understand pull requests, code reviews, and collaborative workflows.

skills.name: Backend Development (Node.js)
skills.description: Server-side development using Node.js to handle business logic, APIs, authentication, and database communication.
skills.notes: Learn Express.js, routing, middleware, REST APIs, handling requests/responses, and building scalable backend services.

skills.name: Databases (SQL & NoSQL)
skills.description: Systems used to store, retrieve, and manage application data efficiently.
skills.notes: Includes PostgreSQL (relational) and Redis (caching). Learn CRUD operations, indexing, relationships, and performance optimization.

skills.name: Authentication & Authorization
skills.description: Mechanisms to verify user identity and control access to resources within an application.
skills.notes: Covers JWT authentication, sessions, role-based access, and security best practices for protecting APIs and user data.

skills.name: DevOps & Cloud Services
skills.description: Practices and tools for deploying, managing, and scaling applications using cloud platforms and automation.
skills.notes: Includes AWS services (EC2, S3, VPC), Linux basics, CI/CD pipelines, and infrastructure tools like Terraform and Ansible.

skills.name: Deployment & Monitoring
skills.description: Processes and tools used to deploy applications and monitor their performance in production environments.
skills.notes: Covers GitHub Actions for CI/CD, application deployment strategies, logging, monitoring tools, and performance tracking to ensure reliability.
